title: Chewy Mexican Corn Tacos
description: These chewy tacos feature charred corn, fragrant marjoram, and a splash of lime, all tucked into corn tortillas and lightly bathed in rendered pork fat for extra flavor.
instructions: Heat a dry skillet over medium heat and toast the corn tortillas for 30 seconds on each side until they puff slightly.
In a separate pan, melt the lard over medium heat and add the corn kernels, stirring until they start to caramelize, about 5 minutes.
Sprinkle the dried marjoram over the corn, season with salt, and continue cooking for another minute, letting the aroma release.
Remove the corn mixture from the pan and let it cool slightly, then transfer to a bowl.
Add the lime juice to the corn, stir well, and taste to adjust salt if needed.
Warm the toasted tortillas in a dry skillet for 10 seconds each side, then spoon the corn mixture onto each tortilla.
Finish each taco with a sprinkle of crumbled cotija cheese and chopped cilantro, if desired.
Serve immediately while the tortillas remain chewy and enjoy.
